title: Forest and Agriculture Finance Initiatives
focus: Tropical Forest Forever Facility (TFFF), RAIZ Accelerator, and Scaling J-REDD+ Coalition
rationale: These three initiatives form a distinct cluster focused on mobilizing finance for tropical forests, restoring degraded farmland, and reducing emissions from deforestation.
sectionExcerpt: Tropical Forest Forever Facility (TFFF): India joined as an Observer. Trustee and Interim host: World Bank. Aims to mobilise $25 billion from member countries and attract up to $100 billion from private investors. Mandates at least 20% of all payments flow directly to Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities. Resilient Agriculture Investment for net-Zero land degradation (RAIZ) Accelerator: Brazil & 10 Partner Nations. Hosted by Brazil under the FAO FAST Partnership. To restore 3 million hectares of degraded farmland. Scaling J-REDD+ Coalition: Offers a credible, mature pathway to mobilize $3-6 billion per year by 2030 to halt and reverse tropical deforestation.
evidenceBullets: TFFF aims to mobilize $25 billion with the World Bank as trustee and India participating as an observer.
TFFF mandates that at least 20% of all payments flow directly to Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities.
RAIZ Accelerator aims to restore 3 million hectares of degraded farmland and is hosted by Brazil under the FAO FAST Partnership.
Scaling J-REDD+ Coalition aims to mobilize $3-6 billion per year by 2030 to halt and reverse tropical deforestation.

title: Climate Finance and Adaptation Frameworks
focus: Baku to Belém Roadmap, NCQG, and Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) frameworks
rationale: This section groups the core COP finance targets (NCQG) and adaptation tracking mechanisms (GGA, Baku Adaptation Roadmap) into a single examinable block.
sectionExcerpt: Baku to Belém Roadmap to 1.3T: The official pathway to mobilize the New Collective Quantified Goal (NCQG) of $1.3 trillion/year by 2035. Tripling of adaptation finance: Increase adaptation finance to $120 billion per year. Measurement framework for tracking adaptation progress under the Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA): Indicators adopted across 7 thematic sectors. Formalizes a four-stage cycle. Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) was established under Article 7.1 of the Paris Agreement (2015). Baku Adaptation Roadmap: Approves and establishes the work for 2026-2028, until the next Global Stocktake of Paris Agreement.
evidenceBullets: Baku to Belém Roadmap to 1.3T is the official pathway to mobilize the NCQG of $1.3 trillion/year by 2035.
The framework targets tripling adaptation finance to $120 billion per year.
The GGA measurement framework adopted indicators across 7 thematic sectors: water, food, health, ecosystems, infrastructure, poverty, and culture.
The Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) was established under Article 7.1 of the Paris Agreement (2015).
Baku Adaptation Roadmap establishes adaptation work for 2026-2028 until the next Global Stocktake.

title: General International Climate Initiatives
focus: FINI, Open Coalition on Compliance Carbon Markets, and Belem Declaration for Green Industrialization
rationale: Covers broad international initiatives focused on adaptation investment pipelines, carbon markets, and green industrialization.
sectionExcerpt: FINI (Fostering Investible National Implementation): Supported by IDB and GCF to make NAPs investible and develop USD 1 trillion in adaptation investment pipelines by 2028. Open Coalition on Compliance Carbon Markets: Initiative to harmonize carbon pricing and integrity standards across national borders and collaborate in defining best practices for MRV. Belem Declaration for Green Industrialization: Accelerating decarbonization of heavy emitting industries and promoting green industrialization.
evidenceBullets: FINI is supported by IDB and GCF to develop USD 1 trillion in adaptation investment pipelines by 2028.
Open Coalition on Compliance Carbon Markets aims to harmonize carbon pricing and integrity standards across national borders.
Belem Declaration for Green Industrialization focuses on decarbonizing heavy emitting industries and promoting green industrialization.

title: Energy and Infrastructure Transitions
focus: Power Grids expansion, PACT, and Belém 4x Pledge on Sustainable Fuels
rationale: Groups initiatives specifically targeting energy transition, grid resilience, and sustainable fuels.
sectionExcerpt: Plan to Accelerate the Expansion and Resilience of Power Grids: Supported by Green Grids Initiative (GGI). Plan to Accelerate Coal Transitions (PACT): Supported by Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA) to fast-track the transition from coal to clean energy. Belém 4x Pledge on Sustainable Fuels: Participants include Italy, Japan, India and Brazil. Objective is to quadruple the use of sustainable fuels (hydrogen, biofuels, e-fuels) by 2035 compared to 2024 levels, with progress tracked by the IEA.
evidenceBullets: Plan to Accelerate the Expansion and Resilience of Power Grids is supported by the Green Grids Initiative (GGI).
Plan to Accelerate Coal Transitions (PACT) is supported by the Powering Past Coal Alliance (PPCA).
Belém 4x Pledge on Sustainable Fuels aims to quadruple the use of sustainable fuels by 2035 compared to 2024 levels.
Progress of the Belém 4x Pledge will be tracked by the International Energy Agency (IEA).

title: Social, Health, and Information Integrity Declarations
focus: Belém Health Action Plan, Gender Action Plan, Information Integrity, and Hunger/Poverty Declarations
rationale: Clusters the social, health, gender, and information-focused declarations adopted at Belém/COP.
sectionExcerpt: Belém Health Action Plan: World's first international climate adaptation plan dedicated to health, with $300 million initial funding to build climate-resilient health sector. Belém Gender Action Plan (GAP): Adopted by Parties at COP30 for 2026-2034 to implement Lima Work Programme on gender. Declaration on Information Integrity on Climate Change: Establishes shared international commitments to address climate disinformation. First time the topic of information integrity has been included in the COP Action Agenda. Belém Declaration on Hunger, Poverty, and People-Centered Climate Action: Signed by 43 countries and the EU to promote actions addressing unequal climate impacts.
evidenceBullets: Belém Health Action Plan is the world's first international climate adaptation plan dedicated to health, backed by $300 million initial funding.
Belém Gender Action Plan (GAP) was adopted for 2026-2034 to implement the Lima Work Programme on gender.
Declaration on Information Integrity on Climate Change marks the first time information integrity is included in the COP Action Agenda.
Belém Declaration on Hunger, Poverty, and People-Centered Climate Action was signed by 43 countries and the EU to address unequal climate impacts on vulnerable populations.
